problem_addressed: Existing nodule segmentation methods require costly and labor-intensive pixel-level annotations, limiting their practical application in ultrasound imaging.
core_claim: A weakly supervised framework leveraging SAM's pseudo-label generation with uncertainty-aware cross-teaching can achieve accurate nodule segmentation using only aspect ratio annotations.
key_contributions: Three types of bounding box prompts generated from clinical shape priors to enhance SAM's segmentation capabilities
SAM-driven uncertainty estimation method using prediction inconsistencies to identify unreliable regions
Uncertainty-aware cross-teaching strategy integrating label-space perturbations to mitigate pseudo-label noise
novelty: Integration of SAM's uncertainty estimation with cross-teaching mechanism using pseudo-labels from shape-prior bounding boxes, specifically designed for ultrasound nodule segmentation under weak supervision.
method_summary: The framework first generates three bounding box prompts (tight box, ellipse-based, and circle-based) from clinical annotations to guide SAM in producing pseudo-labels. It then selects optimal pseudo-labels using intersection/union operations and employs uncertainty-aware cross-teaching between two models trained on these labels.
method_components: Box Prompts Generation (BPG) module creating shape-prior bounding boxes
Pseudo-label selection module using intersection/union operations
SAM-driven uncertainty estimation computing XOR between intersection and union masks
Uncertainty-aware cross-teaching with dual models and label-space perturbations
key_innovation: Using SAM's prediction inconsistencies (via XOR operation between intersection and union of pseudo-labels) to generate uncertainty maps that guide cross-teaching, effectively reducing the impact of pseudo-label inaccuracies during training.
assumptions: Nodules exhibit convex shapes that can be approximated by ellipses and circles
SAM's predictions, despite prompt sensitivity, contain sufficient signal for uncertainty estimation
Cross-teaching between two models can effectively leverage complementary pseudo-label information
theoretical_grounding: None explicitly provided in the text; method relies on empirical validation through experiments on clinical datasets.
datasets_used: Thyroid Ultrasound Dataset (422 left + 422 right images)
Breast Ultrasound Dataset (604 left + 151 right images)
hyperparameters: Batch size: 24, Learning rate: 0.01 with poly learning rate strategy, Image size: 256×256 pixels, Data augmentation: random rotation and flipping, Backbone: UNet, λ (weight factor for cross teaching loss)
headline_result: Ours achieved DSC of 73.5±22.2 and HD95 of 32.8±71.5 on Thyroid Ultrasound, and DSC of 75.5±19.8 and HD95 of 52.3±36.5 on Breast Ultrasound
ablations: Dual Model with Cross Pseudo Supervision (CPS)
Dual Model with Dynamically Mixed Pseudo Labels Supervision (DMPLS)
improvement_over_baseline: Thyroid Ultrasound: DSC improvement of 1.4 (73.5 - 72.1), HD95 improvement of -9.2 (32.8 - 42.0). Breast Ultrasound: DSC improvement of 3.4 (75.5 - 72.1), HD95 improvement of -9.7 (52.3 - 42.0)
unstated_weaknesses: Dependence on SAM foundation model which may not be optimized for medical images
Limited annotation type (only aspect ratio annotations) may not capture complex nodule shapes
Single modality focus (ultrasound) raises questions about cross-modality generalization
Uncertainty estimation mechanism not elaborated in abstract
Lack of comparison with other weakly supervised methods
threats_to_validity: Potential overfitting to specific ultrasound datasets
SAM's performance on medical images may differ significantly from natural images
Quality of aspect ratio annotations could significantly impact performance
Uncertainty estimates may not be calibrated properly for medical applications
reproducibility: low (limited information in abstract, needs full paper details)
open_questions: How exactly is uncertainty quantified and used in the cross teaching process?
What is the computational cost of using SAM for pseudo-label generation?
How does the method perform with different types of nodules or imaging artifacts?
What is the threshold for pseudo-label selection based on uncertainty?
future_work: Extending to other medical imaging modalities
gaps_this_enables: Framework for uncertainty-aware pseudo-label selection in medical imaging
Method for leveraging foundation models with minimal annotations
Cross-teaching paradigm for semi-supervised medical segmentation
relevance_to_topic: Highly relevant as it directly addresses uncertainty-aware pseudo-label selection for semi-supervised medical image tasks.
can_run_on_macbook: unknown (depends on SAM model size and computational requirements)
best_result: Ours achieved DSC of 73.5±22.2 and HD95 of 32.8±71.5 on Thyroid Ultrasound, and DSC of 75.5±19.8 and HD95 of 52.3±36.5 on Breast Ultrasound
title: Sam-Driven Weakly Supervised Nodule Segmentation with Uncertainty-Aware Cross Teaching
